codeReview.summary: The implementation matches the feature requirements: Google OAuth strategy/configuration, requireAuth middleware, session + passport wiring in server/index.ts, auth route mounting, and auth tests were all added. Existing legacy API routes remain unprotected in this feature, consistent with the requirement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill requires strict test-first (RED), but this feature's transcript shows implementation files were created/edited before auth tests were added. Skill guidance may be too rigid for some auth tasks or should be enforced more explicitly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md says "Write Tests First (RED)"; worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session 023e149a-736e-44bc-9214-d4fef48ee259 shows Create/Edit on server/middleware/auth.ts, server/auth.ts, server/index.ts, and server/routes.ts before Create server/__tests__/auth.test.ts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill and mission guidance conflict on TypeScript verification: skill says npm run check must pass, while mission AGENTS documents pre-existing TypeScript failures and instructs workers to avoid introducing new errors.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md step 4 says "Run npm run check ... must pass"; mission AGENTS.md states "npm run check fails with 10 pre-existing TypeScript errors" and worker handoff for 023e149a-736e-44bc-9214-d4fef48ee259 reports this exact mismatch.

summary: Reviewed commit 1cf847e for google-oauth-auth and found it functionally complete for the specified scope with no blocking or non-blocking code defects identified. Added two shared-state skill observations about TDD procedure drift and npm run check guidance mismatch.
